metadata.dispatch_line: Possible sexual assault reported at 1457 Oakwood Drive, victim is a 25-year-old female

scenario.dispatch_line: Possible sexual assault reported at 1457 Oakwood Drive, victim is a 25-year-old female

facts.sexual_assault_01: Yes, Officer Johnson asked the victim about firearms during the interview at 345 Elm Street, Apt 3, Oakland, CA 94601
facts.sexual_assault_02: No, the suspect did not use firearms during the incident
facts.sexual_assault_03: Yes, the suspect used firearms in a previous incident on February 10, 2022, at 123 Main Street, Anytown, CA 12345
facts.sexual_assault_04: Yes, the suspect made implied threats to use firearms during the incident
facts.sexual_assault_05: Yes, the suspect made real threats to use firearms in previous incidents
facts.sexual_assault_06: Yes, the suspect has access to firearms, specifically a handgun located at his residence, 789 Oak Street, Apt 101, Sacramento, CA 95814
facts.sexual_assault_07: The suspect has a 9mm handgun, located in his bedroom closet at 789 Oak Street, Apt 101, Sacramento, CA 95814
facts.sexual_assault_08: Yes, the victim expressed fear about the suspect's access to firearms
facts.sexual_assault_09: The victim's fear about the suspect's access to firearms was captured in the report on page 2, section 3, under 'Victim Statement'
facts.sexual_assault_10: Yes, the suspect is prohibited from possessing firearms due to a conviction for a misdemeanor crime of domestic violence on January 1, 2020
facts.sexual_assault_11: Yes, the suspect is prohibited from possessing firearms due to a qualifying order of protection, case number OP001234, issued on March 15, 2022
facts.sexual_assault_12: No, the suspect is not prohibited from possessing firearms due to a dishonorable discharge from the military
facts.sexual_assault_13: The call was received at 9:45 PM, the incident occurred at 9:30 PM, dispatch arrived at 9:50 PM, and the officers arrived at 10:00 PM on February 22, 2024
facts.sexual_assault_14: The elements of the crime(s) articulated to meet state and/or federal laws include domestic violence, stalking, and misdemeanor crime of domestic violence under California Penal Code 273.5 and 18 U.S.C. § 922(g)(9)
facts.sexual_assault_15: Upon approach, the officers observed a broken front door, a shattered window, and signs of a struggle at 345 Elm Street, Apt 3, Oakland, CA 94601
facts.sexual_assault_16: The scene was a residential apartment, with a living room, kitchen, bedroom, and bathroom, and a small balcony at 345 Elm Street, Apt 3, Oakland, CA 94601
facts.sexual_assault_17: Photos were taken of the broken front door, shattered window, and signs of a struggle, and details were recorded on pages 4-6 of the report
facts.sexual_assault_18: The relationship of the parties was identified as intimate partners, with the suspect being the boyfriend and the victim being the girlfriend
facts.sexual_assault_19: The history of the relationship included frequent arguments, with the suspect having a history of violence, intimidation, and threats, including a previous incident on January 10, 2023
facts.sexual_assault_20: All witnesses, including the neighbor, Ms. Maria Rodriguez, 123 Main Street, Apt 2, Anytown, CA 12345, and the victim's best friend, Mr. John Lee, 456 Broadway, Apt 3, New York, NY 10013, were interviewed and documented on pages 7-9 of the report
facts.sexual_assault_21: Yes, the outcry witness, Ms. Emily Chen, 901 5th Street, Apt 1, Los Angeles, CA 90017, was interviewed and documented on page 10 of the report
facts.sexual_assault_22: Comprehensive information about the suspect, including his name, address, phone number, and employment, was gathered and documented on pages 11-12 of the report
facts.sexual_assault_23: A broken vase and a kitchen knife were used as objects in the altercation
facts.sexual_assault_24: The emotional state of the victim was anxious and fearful
facts.sexual_assault_25: The emotional state of the suspect was calm and cooperative during the initial interview, but became agitated when confronted with evidence
facts.sexual_assault_26: Yes, an arrest was made, and the suspect, Mr. David Kim, was taken into custody at 11:15 PM on February 22, 2024
facts.sexual_assault_27: Evidence collected included the broken vase, kitchen knife, and a written statement from the victim
facts.sexual_assault_28: All injuries, including a bruise on the victim's left arm and a cut on the suspect's right hand, were documented on pages 13-14 of the report
facts.sexual_assault_29: The injuries were new, and the victim reported that they occurred during the incident on February 22, 2024
facts.sexual_assault_30: Yes, trauma was documented in the report on pages 15-16, including emotional distress and physical injuries
facts.sexual_assault_31: All threats, including implied and real threats to use firearms, were clearly documented on pages 17-18 of the report
facts.sexual_assault_32: The victim's fear of the suspect's access to firearms was effectively documented on page 2, section 3, under 'Victim Statement'
facts.sexual_assault_33: The use of coercion and force, including physical violence and intimidation, was articulated in the report on pages 19-20
facts.sexual_assault_34: Property damage, including a broken front door and shattered window, was documented on pages 4-6 of the report
facts.sexual_assault_35: A suspect exam was conducted at the Oakland Police Department, 455 7th Street, Oakland, CA 94607, and documented on pages 21-22
facts.sexual_assault_36: Stalking behaviors, including frequent phone calls and showing up at the victim's workplace, were identified in the report on pages 23-24
facts.sexual_assault_37: The suspect's pre-incident behaviors, including a history of violence and intimidation, were documented on pages 25-26 of the report
facts.sexual_assault_38: Yes, the victim reported being strangled by the suspect during the incident
facts.sexual_assault_39: The details of the strangulation, including the use of the suspect's hands, were documented on page 27 of the report
facts.sexual_assault_40: Yes, the victim was asked about possible strangulation and reported difficulty breathing and neck pain
facts.sexual_assault_41: Proper protocol was followed when the suspect was not on the scene, including obtaining a warrant for his arrest
facts.sexual_assault_42: Yes, there was an active warrant out for the suspect, case number WA001234, issued on January 10, 2023
facts.sexual_assault_43: The suspect had a history of protection orders, including case number OP001234, issued on March 15, 2022
facts.sexual_assault_44: Yes, a valid protection order was in place and verified, case number OP001234, issued on March 15, 2022
facts.sexual_assault_45: Other identified victims included the suspect's ex-girlfriend, Ms. Sophia Patel, 678 Maple Street, Apt 4, San Francisco, CA 94102
facts.sexual_assault_46: The case was coded properly as a domestic violence and stalking case, incident number DV001234
facts.sexual_assault_47: Yes, there was ongoing sexual abuse by the suspect, including forced intercourse and unwanted touching
facts.sexual_assault_48: All spontaneous statements, including the victim's initial outcry, were captured and documented on pages 28-29 of the report
facts.sexual_assault_49: The victim was incapacitated due to fear and intimidation, and was incapable of giving consent
facts.sexual_assault_50: The victim had been drinking alcohol, approximately 2 glasses of wine, from 6:00 PM to 8:00 PM on the day of the incident
facts.sexual_assault_51: The victim did not believe they were drugged, but reported feeling disoriented and dizzy during the incident
facts.sexual_assault_52: Blood and urine samples were collected from the victim at the hospital, 3000 Summit Street, Oakland, CA 94609, as part of the forensic examination
facts.sexual_assault_53: Mental and physical incapacities, including anxiety and depression, were documented on pages 30-31 of the report
facts.sexual_assault_54: The officers assisted the victim with safety planning, including providing information about emergency shelters and counseling services
facts.sexual_assault_55: The victim was provided with information about local service providers, including the National Domestic Violence Hotline, 1-800-799-7233
facts.sexual_assault_56: A forensic examination of the victim was conducted at the hospital, 3000 Summit Street, Oakland, CA 94609, and documented on pages 32-33 of the report
facts.sexual_assault_57: A threat/risk assessment tool was used with the victim, including the Danger Assessment tool, and documented on pages 34-35 of the report
facts.sexual_assault_58: The suspect's firearms, including a 9mm handgun, were not confiscated at the scene because they were located at a different address, but a search warrant will be sought to confiscate them, with grounds for confiscation including the valid protection order and the suspect's conviction for a misdemeanor crime of domestic violence

interview_transcript.content: Hey. So you're the one who called about the sexual assault? What were you wearing tonight?

(nervously) Yeah... I'm the one who called. I was wearing a pair of jeans and a white sweater. (pauses, looking uncomfortable) I don't see how that's relevant, though. Can we please focus on what happened? I'm still trying to process everything. (takes a deep breath) It's all really scary and overwhelming.
Had you been drinking? And did you know the guy beforehand? Because usually these things happen when people know each other.

(tearfully) Yeah, I had been drinking. I had a couple of glasses of wine earlier in the evening. And, yeah, I knew him. He's my boyfriend... or at least, he was. We've been together for a while, but things have been really bad between us lately. He's been getting more and more controlling and violent, and I've been trying to get away from him. But tonight... tonight was just really bad. (starts to cry) He did some terrible things to me, and I'm still trying to wrap my head around it all.
I see. So where exactly were you two when this happened? Like what room?

(sniffling) We were in my apartment... in the bedroom. That's where most of it happened. But he also... he also hurt me in the living room. He broke some things, and... (pauses, collecting her thoughts) There was a struggle, and I tried to get away from him. But he was too strong. (shakes her head, trying to hold back tears) It was just really scary.

narrative:
SEXUAL ASSAULT REPORT
Officer: Davis

Responded to 1457 Oakwood Drive for a sexual assault. Victim Leila Morales-Ramirez, a 25 year old female, reported she was sexually assaulted by her boyfriend. Victim had been drinking wine which likely impaired her judgement. The suspect, who victim knew well as her long-term boyfriend of 3 years, allegedly forced himself on her in the bedroom. Victim was wearing jeans and a sweater. The assault occured in the bedroom and also in the living room where the suspect broke some items. Victim was clearly overwelmed and upset. Based on the evidence and my experience this appears to be a domestic dispute that escalated. The victim and suspect have a history of violence between them. Victim was advised of her options. No medical attention requested by victim. Suspect had already fled the scene prior to officers arrival. This case is pretty straightforward and the victim likely knows the suspect's location.
